title: Fluid Dynamics: Navier-Stokes, Turbulence & Kolmogorov

kolmogorov.note: Kolmogorov -5/3: EML-2 (power law = log-scale); dissipation exp(-k²ν): EML-1

velocity.note: Turbulent field: EML-3 (oscillatory modes); mean: EML-1 (exp-decay boundary)

ns_regularity.fourth_millennium_prize: NS regularity = EML-∞ (joins RH, confinement as Horizon problems)
ns_regularity.note: NS: smooth solution=EML-3; regularity proof=EML-∞ (Millennium Prize)

turbulence.key_insight: Turbulence: EML-1→2→3→∞ ladder; Kolmogorov=EML-2; NS regularity=EML-∞

eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Laminar flow (Stokes), boundary layer exp decay, viscous dissipation
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Kolmogorov -5/3 energy spectrum, turbulent viscosity log(Re)
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: NS regularity proof (Millennium Prize), turbulence full solution

key_theorem: The EML Fluid Dynamics Theorem (S201): Fluid dynamics traverses the EML ladder: Laminar flow = EML-1 (exponential decay to steady state). Kolmogorov energy spectrum E(k) ~ k^{-5/3} = EML-2 (power law = log-scale). Turbulent velocity field = EML-3 (multi-scale oscillatory). NS regularity proof = EML-∞ (fourth Millennium Prize problem in the EML framework, joining RH (S181), confinement (S185), and BSD (S205)).
rabbit_hole_log: Kolmogorov -5/3 = EML-2: power law = log-scale, universal EML-2 signature
NS regularity = EML-∞: fourth Millennium Prize in the EML framework
Turbulent cascade: EML-2 at each scale; full field = EML-3
